item.title: Schecter Reaper-5 bass in Natural Satin — $1,499
item.description:
$1,499 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Schecter Reaper-5 Electric Bass Natural Satin

The Reaper-5 delivers a slim, fast-playing experience thanks to its thin "C" neck profile and 35" scale length, making it comfortable for extended sessions while maintaining excellent low-B clarity. The figured poplar burl top and natural satin finish create striking visual appeal without sacrificing the resonant tonal foundation of the swamp ash body. This is a serious instrument for players who want both aesthetic presence and genuine sonic versatility.

Playability & Build

The maple neck features walnut stringers and carbon fiber reinforcement for exceptional stability, paired with a 16" radius ebony fingerboard and 24 jumbo frets that facilitate fast, precise playing. The bolt-on construction combined with the Schecter Diamond Custom Bass Bridge (string-through or top-load capable) yields bright, articulate tones with solid sustain, while the Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut (45mm) and TonePros "M Series" tuners ensure reliable tuning stability across all five strings.

Tone & Electronics

The 18V Darkglass Tone Capsule EQ provides three-band tone shaping with selectable frequency contours for bass, mid, and upper-mid ranges, giving you extensive control over your sound. The Schecter USA M-50 bridge pickup delivers aggressive, punchy tones while the P-50 neck pickup offers warm, smooth character—both with smoke bobbins for tonal definition and clarity across the frequency spectrum.

Key Features
  • Swamp ash body with figured poplar burl top
  • Maple neck with walnut stringers and carbon fiber reinforcement
  • Thin "C" neck shape for fast, comfortable playability
  • 35" scale length for extended low-B string tension and clarity
  • 16" radius ebony fingerboard with 24 jumbo frets
  • Pearloid offset dot inlays with white side dots
  • Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut, 45mm width
  • 2-way adjustable truss rod with 5/32" (4mm) Allen nut
  • Schecter USA M-50 bridge pickup and P-50 neck pickup
  • 18V Darkglass Tone Capsule EQ with 3-band boost/cut at selectable frequencies
  • Schecter Diamond Custom Bass Bridge with string-through or top-load capability
  • TonePros "M Series" tuners and black and chrome hardware

The Reaper-5 combines premium construction, active electronics with genuine tone-shaping depth, and a visually distinctive design that performs as well as it looks.

item.title: Jackson X Series RR-B IV Limited Edition 4 String Bass - CUSTOM ART BASS — $699.99
item.description:
$699.99 · Excellent · Pirate Sam's Custom Art Guitars

This is a BRAND new overstock bass that was sent from Fender (JACKSON ) to MIRC for distribution to MIRC dealers of which I am one . It was inspected and setup before it shipped to me . I only found a minor finsh flaw on it that was the reason Fender sold it off to MIRC . It plays like new with no issues . I then took it and sanded off all the top black finish down to bare wood then added the inked graphics as shown followed by several coats of semi gloss clear to protect the art work . This is a one off NON factory finish . It is an art guitar by Pirate Sam ( Sam McCready )
I call this one " Color 4 Trees "


Weighs in at  Lbs 5 oz
Year of Production: 2025

The Jackson X Series RR-B IV limited-edition electric bass guitar brings metal’s most iconic silhouette into the low-end realm with unapologetic aggression and refined playability. Built around the legendary Rhoads body shape, this four-string bass commands attention with its angular design, gloss black finish and sharp white pinstripes.

Beneath the sinister looks lies serious engineering: a poplar body with neck-thru maple construction reinforced with graphite for unparalleled sustain and stability. The amaranth fingerboard’s 12"–16" compound radius, jumbo frets and 34" scale deliver smooth, responsive performance across the neck, while Jackson’s signature pointy headstock completes the unmistakable aesthetic. Add the EMG 35DC active humbucker for uncompromising punch and clarity, and a rock-solid hardtail bridge for maximum sustain, and you’ve got a bass that blends legendary style with the brutal tone and reliability today’s heavy players demand.


Specs:

Body

  • Body style: Rhoads
  • Body wood: Poplar
  • Body finish: Gloss

Neck

  • Neck wood: Maple
  • Neck construction: Neck-thru-body with graphite reinforcement and scarf joint
  • Neck finish: Gloss
  • Scale length: 34"
  • Fingerboard wood: Amaranth
  • Fingerboard radius: 12"–16" compound
  • Number of frets: 21
  • Inlays: Pearloid sharkfin
  • Truss rod: Dual-action head-adjust

Hardware

  • Bridge: Jackson hardtail
  • Nut material: Black plastic
  • Nut width: 1.625"
  • Tuning machines: Jackson sealed die-cast
  • Hardware finish: Black

Electronics

  • Pickup configuration: H
  • Bridge pickup: EMG 35DC humbucker
  • Controls: Master volume, master tone

item.title: Gibson Victory Standard Bass 1981 - 1985 - Candy Apple Red — $900
item.description:
$900 · Good · Metz Music

For sale from Metz Music is this super cool Gibson Victory Standard 4 string bass! These basses were manufactured in the early 80s, and never quite took off for Gibson. These unique instruments have a 34" scale, rosewood fretboard and a single Series VIII-B humbucking pickup. The Candy Apple Red finish on this one has faded a bit, but has a great patina! This is rare and vintage, so don't miss your chance to own this one! We have photographed this baby to the best of our abilities to show finish imperfections. Everything on this bass works great, but it would definitely benefit from a fret level/refret. This one weighs in a 10lb 11oz. Please ask any questions you may have!

Ships professionally packaged and fully insured.
We love to talk gear so let us know if you have questions.
At Metz Music we appreciate your patronage!

item.title: Schecter Omen Extreme-4 Electric Bass in Vintage Sunburst Finish — $649
item.description:
$649 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Schecter Omen Extreme-4 Electric Bass in Vintage Sunburst Finish

The Omen Extreme-4 delivers a slim, comfortable playing experience thanks to its thin "C" neck profile and manageable body weight from the mahogany construction with flamed maple top. The 34" scale and 24 X-Jumbo frets provide plenty of real estate for technique work and fast passages without excessive hand strain. This right-handed four-string is built for serious players who demand responsive playability and articulate tone in a compact, aesthetically refined package.

Playability & Construction

The maple neck with rosewood fretboard and thin "C" profile (.787" at the 1st fret, .866" at the 12th fret) sits naturally in the hand, while the 24 X-Jumbo frets and 16" fretboard radius accommodate both precision work and full-hand vibrato techniques. Six bolt-on construction with black chrome hardware ensures rock-solid neck stability and a clean, modern aesthetic that complements the vintage sunburst finish and crème multi-ply binding.

Tone & Electronics

Dual Schecter Diamond Bass humbucking pickups deliver focused, articulate midrange with controlled low-end response—ideal for cutting through a mix in live and studio settings. The active 2-band EQ with Master Volume and Blend controls powered by a 9-volt clip-in battery gives you broad tonal flexibility, from punchy attack to warm, compressed sustain, without requiring permanent modification to the instrument.

Key Features
  • Mahogany body with flamed maple top
  • Maple neck with rosewood fretboard
  • 34" scale (863mm)
  • Thin "C" neck shape with .787"/.866" thickness at 1st/12th fret
  • 24 X-Jumbo frets with 16" fretboard radius
  • Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut, 1.496" width
  • Abalone & Pearloid Vector inlays
  • Two Schecter Diamond Bass humbucking pickups
  • Active 2-Band EQ with Master Volume and Blend controls
  • 9-volt clip-in battery compartment
  • Diamond Bass bridge with black chrome hardware
  • 6 bolt-on construction with crème multi-ply binding
  • Schecter sealed tuners
  • 2-way adjustable truss rod

The Omen Extreme-4 combines professional-grade playability, articulate electronics, and distinctive vintage sunburst aesthetics into a versatile instrument that performs equally well in rock, metal, and progressive music contexts.

item.title: Fender Squier Series Precision Bass 1994-95 Made in Mexico Torino Red w/ Fender Molded Hard Case 1994 - Torino Red — $500
item.description:
$500 · Very Good · Music Go Round Toledo

Condition: Excellent


Estimated Year: 1994–1995


Made In: Mexico


Includes Case/Gig Bag: Fender Molded Hard Case


Accessories/Disclaimer:

Only accessories specifically listed in the Includes Case/Gig Bag section or shown in the photos are included. No additional accessories, software, cables, power supplies, manuals, boxes, or other items are included unless pictured or stated.


Used Fender Squier Series Precision Bass Made in Mexico Torino Red w/ Fender Molded Hard Case


The mid-'90s Fender "Squier Series" Precision Basses have become increasingly desirable among players looking for authentic Fender feel and classic Precision Bass tone at a great value. Built in Fender's Ensenada, Mexico factory, these instruments feature the same comfortable body and neck construction found on many Mexican Fender models from the era while carrying the distinctive "Squier Series" designation.


Finished in classic Torino Red with a rosewood fingerboard, this bass delivers the unmistakable punch, warmth, and simplicity that has made the Precision Bass an industry standard for decades. Whether you're playing rock, country, blues, punk, or worship music, it sits perfectly in the mix.


Features:

• 1994–1995 Fender Squier Series Precision Bass

• Made in Mexico

• Torino Red gloss finish

• Rosewood fingerboard

• Split Precision Bass pickup

• Master Volume and Master Tone controls

• Chrome 4-saddle bridge

• 34" scale length

• Includes Fender molded hard case


Condition:

This bass is in excellent used condition and has been fully tested. The finish shows only light cosmetic wear consistent with careful use, with a nice gloss throughout. The neck is clean and straight, the frets have plenty of life remaining, and the electronics function properly. The hardware is clean overall, and the bass includes what appears to be an upgraded roller string tree on the headstock. The included Fender molded hard case is in good working condition and provides excellent protection for transport and storage.


Store Policies:

• All items are tested before listing and again prior to shipping.

• Ships the next business day.

• Shipping available within the Continental U.S. only.

• We do not accept trades on shipped items.

• Returns accepted within 48 hours of delivery. Buyer is responsible for return shipping, and a 20% restocking fee applies.

• Please message us with any questions before purchasing.

item.title: Squier Limited Edition Classic Vibe '60s Jazz Bass Burgundy Mist 2025 w/ Mods — $565.08
item.description:
$565.08 · Very Good · Sell Us Your Guitar

Here for sale we have a Squier Limited Edition Classic Vibe '60s Jazz Bass in Burgundy Mist finish. Made in Indonesia in 2025, this bass consists of a Poplar body, a Maple neck, and a 20-fret Indian Laurel fingerboard. Other appointments include an upgraded Badass Bass II bridge, open-gear vintage-style tuners, and an upgraded set of DiMarzio DP123 Creme pickups. These pickups are wired to dual volume controls, and a master tone control. 


The Maple neck sits comfortably in the hand, with the ‘C' profile allowing for comfortable reach in all registers and promoting ergonomic thumb positioning, whilst the sanded finish is fantastic for smooth, quick transitions up and down the neck. The Indian Laurel fingerboard feels pleasant under the fingers, whilst the 9.5” radius and narrow tall frets allow for ease of different techniques such as slap bass or chordal playing. The double cutaway and contoured body design allows the player to have unobstructed access to the entire register, whilst also making longer durations of playing a comfortable experience.


The upgraded DiMarzio DP123 Creme single coil pickups sound very traditional, clearly having been vintage-inspired, giving you a deep and punchy tone. The dual volume controls allow you to seamlessly blend the pickups to taste without even needing to touch the tone pot, great for taking you from muted, thumping lows on the neck pickup to full on Jazz bass punch and bite at the bridge, with everything in between.


Brand - Squier

Model - Classic Vibe '60s Jazz Bass

Finish - Burgundy Mist

Year - 2025

Made In - Indonesia

Body - Poplar

Neck - Maple

Neck Profile - 'C' Shape

Fingerboard - Indian Laurel

Frets - 20, Narrow Tall

Nut - Bone

Scale Length- 34"

Nut Width - 1.51"

Fingerboard Radius - 9.5"

Pickups - DiMarzio DP123 Creme Set

Controls - Dual volumes, master tone


Luthier Report


Our ID: 1020626212880

Weight: 9.35lbs  

Serial: ICSG25017070


Aesthetic Condition:

Headstock: Overall excellent condition. All aspects of the headstock display light signs of past use.

Neck: Overall very good condition. A previous owner has sanded the lacquer finish off the rear of the neck. All other aspects of the neck and fingerboard display light signs of past use.

Body: Overall excellent condition. All aspects of the body display light signs of past use.


Condition of Frets:

The frets demonstrate very light signs of use, not affecting playability.  There is moderate material remaining for any fret work the new owner may wish to perform.


Current Set Up:

Truss Rod: Fully functional. Truss rod tool (4mm hex) not included.

Hardware: Overall excellent condition. A previous owner has swapped out the original pick guard and bridge for a pearloid pick guard and a Badass Bass ll bridge, the originals are not included.

Structural: No concerns or issues to note.

Height of the string from the 12th Fret-2mm - Bass 2.5mm

Please be aware that the action on a guitar may move slightly in transit and this measurement is the current height.

Strings on Guitar - 45-100


Electrics: Fully functional. A previous owner has replaced the original pickups with a set of DiMarzio Model J pickups, the original pickups are not included.


Case & Accessories

No case is provided with this guitar. 


Please be aware that for international buyers import fees are not included in our postage and packaging cost, this only covers the cost of the guitar being sent to you. Import fees will be charged by Customs upon the guitar's arrival in the recipient country. Due to Brexit, this also applies to EU buyers. We advise checking the total import cost with Customs as this is not information we are able to accurately provide.

item.title: Yamaha PB600R 1985 - Vintage bass — $1,794.35
item.description:
$1,794.35 · Good · Jean-Baptiste Roche

Ultra-rare collector's item.

100% original

Produced exclusively in 1985 for the Japanese Domestic Market (JDM), available only in red or black. This model represents the high-end pinnacle of the Yamaha PB series.

• Body: Precision shape

• Neck: Jazz Bass profile (very slim)

• Bridge Pickup: Jazz Bass single coil

• Neck Pickup: Splittable humbucker

• Electronics: Fully passive

Versatility & Tone

The humbucker features a push/pull potentiometer, allowing you to switch instantly between a thick, high-output double-coil sound and a more scooped single-coil tone. This bass offers an infinite tonal palette, ranging from classic P-Bass and Jazz Bass vibes to a very "Alembic-esque" hi-fi sound when using both pickups together.

Condition & Setup

• Maintenance: Freshly serviced by a professional luthier.

• Strings: Currently strung with Ernie Ball Light strings (95-40).

• Condition: "Player" condition with honest wear from use.

• Case: Sold with a flight case.

item.description:
$23,922.33 · Excellent · Some Neck Guitars

2004 Alembic Series I 5-String Custom Order Cocobolo, in virtually mint condition. Breathtaking really can't even sum up this exceptional instrument. The craftsmanship is beyond anything we've seen, expertly build with only the best woods - the exact reason Alembic basses are so coveted after and have been since their inception in the late '60's. 

This custom order has some seriously cool specs, this one is a Series I with spec'd with Standard Point body with Triple Omega style handle cutaway. Cocobolo Top & Back laminate. Mahogany body wood with Maple accent wood. 7 Piece Maple & Ebony neck. Cocobolo Veneers. Series II electronics system. Custom feature Chrome Plated Hardware.

We're absolutely floored by this one. Custom ordered this would be easily $45k+ - a seriously high end machine.

Includes OHSC, cable, DS-5 Power Supply and pictured paraphernalia.

Feel free to get in touch for a more accurate shipping quote to your location, or for any other information - happy to help!

item.title: Music Man StingRay 50th Anniversary 4 string Bass in Molten Gold — $5,084.27
item.description:
$5,084.27 · Brand New · Project Music

Legendary Stingray Turns 50 - Celebrating 50 years of the iconic StingRay, these Golden Anniversary editions pay tribute to five decades of innovation, tone, and unmistakable style. Built with resonant poplar bodies, they deliver a balanced, responsive low-end growl with exceptional clarity and sustain. The roasted maple necks are paired with sleek ebony fretboards featuring elegant gold dot inlays, while glow-in-the-dark side dots provide precision and visibility on any stage. Finished in a striking high-gloss buff, these commemorative StingRays blend heritage craftsmanship with forward-looking performance—celebrating half a century of punch, clarity, and authority.

The StingRay 4’s tight low end, punchy mids, and unmistakable attack have defined modern bass for five decades. Its legendary 34" scale delivers the perfect balance of tension, clarity, and feel—producing a focused, articulate response that cuts through any mix. These Golden Anniversary editions celebrate that enduring legacy with powerful neodymium-powered pickups, delivering increased output, expanded frequency range, and the dynamic headroom that has made the StingRay sound instantly recognizable around the world.

50th Anniversary Bridge - Featuring a stamped 50th Anniversary gold-plated steel plate with matching gold-plated stainless steel saddles, this top-loading bridge enhances string attack and resonance, delivering the bright, punchy tone and sustain that have defined the Music Man Stingray sound for decades.

item.title: Ibanez PCBE12MH — $302.85
item.description:
$302.85 · Brand New · Dijkmans Guitars

De compacte akoestische bas die overal meegaat en altijd klinkt

De Ibanez PCBE12MH is een akoestische basgitaar die speciaal is ontworpen voor bassisten die ook zonder versterker willen spelen. Het grand concert-formaat maakt hem lekker handzaam, terwijl de okoumé top, rug en zijkanten zorgen voor een warme, volle toon met voldoende body. De open pore naturel afwerking geeft hem een eerlijk, organisch karakter dat past bij het instrument.

Dankzij de ingebouwde pickup en preamp is de PCBE12MH ook direct aan te sluiten op een versterker of PA. De maple hals met laurel fretboard speelt soepel en comfortabel, met 20 frets op een schaallengte van 32" (medium scale). Dat maakt hem geschikt voor zowel beginners als bassisten die op zoek zijn naar een lichte, veelzijdige akoestische bas voor thuis, op reis of op het podium.

At first, please feel free to order as it is.

Product Details

The Classic Vibe '70s Jazz Bass 5st is a 5-string bass that combines the look of a 1970s Jazz Bass with an accessible design.

It balances a classic look with practical playability, making it an easy-to-use model suitable for a wide range of uses, from stage to recording.

This model features a 34-inch scale, 9.5-inch radius fingerboard, narrow-tall frets, and a vintage-style bridge.

It's equipped with two Fender Designed Alnico single-coil pickups, and its simple controls (two volume and one tone) allow for straightforward shaping of a classic Jazz Bass sound.

When plugged into an amplifier, it delivers a well-defined attack and the extended low end characteristic of a 5-string bass. Fingerstyle playing maintains thickness while projecting a strong core tone.

Mixing in the bridge pickup creates a tight, clear tone, while focusing on the neck pickup makes it easy to create rounded bass lines.

This model is perfect for players who prefer a classic look while also wanting extended low-end frequencies. This guitar possesses a straightforwardness that makes it suitable for a variety of genres, making it a great choice for both a first 5-string guitar and a backup instrument.

Please consider it!

[Specifications]
Body: Soft Maple
Neck: Maple
Fingerboard: Maple
Fingerboard Radius: 9.5 inch-R
Nut Width: 38.1 mm
String Spacing: 18 mm
Scale Length: 34 inch
Number of Frets: 20 F
Pickups: Fender Designed Alnico Single-Coil
Controls: 2-Vol, 1-Tone

[Condition]

Fret remaining: Approximately 90%
Truss rod: Plenty of adjustment range
Electronics: No problems
Weight: 4.67 kg

item.title: MUSIC MAN StingRay 2004 — $1,607.50
item.description:
$1,607.50 · Very Good · Qsic - secondhand musical instruments store from Japan -

Welcome to Qsic's Reverb Shop!
1. The description texts have been generated from our Japanese site by automatic translation.
Instrument or music terms are often not translated correctly, so please let us know if you have any questions.

2. For orders from countries or regions not covered by EMS or FedEx, the difference in shipping costs will be billed again via PayPal after your order is placed.
At first, please feel free to order as it is.

Product Details

The MUSIC MAN StingRay is a representative 4-string active bass guitar from Music Man, a brand that has been around since the 1970s.

With its large humbucker pickup positioned near the bridge and its powerful, forward-projecting character, it's a classic model widely used in genres from rock to funk.

This model features an ash body, maple neck, and rosewood fingerboard, with a 34-inch scale and 21 frets.

It's equipped with a Music Man Humbucking Pickup and a 9V Active 3-Band EQ preamp.

With a single volume control and treble, middle, and bass controls, its simple configuration makes it easy to adjust the low-end thickness and high-end definition.

When plugged into an amplifier, it delivers a fast attack and a thick, solid bass tone that projects clearly.

The sound's definition is not easily blurred, and it's easy to create a sustained, forward sound when fingerpicking and a bright, clear tone when slapping.

The density of the humbucker pickups and the wide range of adjustment of the 3-band EQ allow you to aim for a sound that won't get lost in a band mix.

For those seeking the distinctive presence unique to StingRay, this model combines ease of use with a strong presence.

Recommended for those who want to build a reliable sound in live settings, centered around the clear response characteristic of a 4-string active bass.

Please consider this opportunity.

Product Details

We've received a shipment of the SR-305E MGB, a highly practical model featuring an arched body that conforms easily to the body and a slim neck, combined with an okoume body, a 5-piece maple and walnut neck, and a jatoba fingerboard.

This instrument has a 34-inch scale and 24 frets, providing a wide range of tones for a 5-string bass. It features a 45mm nut width, a 305-inch radius fingerboard, 1 volume control, balancer, 3-band EQ, and a 3-way switch, making it easy to achieve a wide range of sounds.

When plugged into an amplifier, it delivers the rich bass tone characteristic of a 5-string bass, along with the responsiveness and cohesive sound typical of the SR series. Fingerstyle playing brings out the bass definition clearly, while pick playing emphasizes the attack. The EQ offers a wide range of tonal possibilities, from a midrange that won't get lost in a band mix to a full, rich low end.

This model is ideal for those who want to leverage the range of a 5-string bass while enjoying light handling and versatile sound shaping. This guitar is designed to be versatile, suitable for practice, live performances, and recording, without being limited to a specific genre.

Please consider this opportunity.

YAMAHA TRBX605 FM

This model is a top-of-the-line 5-string bass in Yamaha's TRBX series, offering a balanced combination of sound quality, playability, and functionality demanded by modern players.

The beautiful flame maple top exudes a sense of luxury, making it visually striking on stage.

The body features a laminated construction combining alder and maple, achieving a balance between rich mid-low frequencies and clear, articulate high frequencies.

The 3-band EQ, switchable between active and passive modes, allows for versatile sound shaping to suit various genres and playing styles.

Despite being a 5-string bass, this instrument achieves excellent balance and comfortable playability, responding reliably to all playing techniques, from slap and fingerstyle to pick playing.

The excellent response of the low B string, in particular, provides reliability for stage and recording use.

This is a reliable instrument not only as a main instrument for daily performances, but also for bassists who want to handle a wide range of musical genres.

item.title: Fender 75th Anniversary Player II Precision Bass, Diamond Dust Sparkle — $1,340.64
item.description:
$1,340.64 · Brand New · Project Music

This rather special celebratory Diamond Dust Sparkle P Bass is part of the highly regarded Player II range, and as such it combines performance orientated features with value, and in so doing cerates a more individual 4 string bass guitar that is hard to resist. We especially like the 75th Anniversary Thunderbolt Split Single-Coil Precision Bass pickup and wonderful eye catching but not over the top finish.

The Player II Modified Active Precision Bass® is a classic bass with the modern player in mind. Everything about the neck is designed for fast and fluid playability, from the Modern “C”-profile with silky satin urethane finish on the back to the comfy 9.5”-radius slab rosewood or 1-piece maple fingerboard with smooth rolled edges and 20 medium jumbo frets. The alder body provides classic Fender punch and is available in a variety of exciting new finishes. The Player II Modified Precision Bass® Neck and Noiseless™ Jazz Bass® Bridge Pickups deliver classic Fender thump and growl while eliminating unwanted noise and the all-new active preamp with passive tone control and improved EQ section gives players the perfect tool to dial in their favorite tones. The 4-saddle HiMass™ bridge, tapered-shaft tuners and TUSQ® nut all combine to keep tuning incredibly stable for even the most demanding performances.

  • Product Name: Fender 75th Anniversary Player II Precision Bass RW, Diamond Dust Sparkle
  • Product Code: 0140780313

Body

  • Body Material: Alder
  • Body Shape: Precision Bass
  • Finish: Gloss Polyester, Diamond Dust Sparkle
  • Pickguard: 4-Ply Aged White Pearl

Neck & Fretboard

  • Neck Material: Maple
  • Neck Profile: Modern "C"
  • Neck Finish: Satin Urethane (Back), Gloss Urethane (Front)
  • Scale Length: 34" (864 mm)
  • Fingerboard Material: Rosewood
  • Fingerboard Radius: 9.5" (241 mm)
  • Frets: 20 Medium Jumbo
  • Inlays: White Dot
  • Nut Material: Bone
  • Nut Width: 1.625" (41.3 mm)
  • Truss Rod: Single Action, Head Adjust

Hardware & Electronics

  • Bridge: 4-Saddle Standard with Single-Groove Steel “Barrel” Saddles
  • Tuners: Standard Open-Gear
  • Hardware Finish: Nickel/Chrome
  • Pickup Configuration: Split Single-Coil
  • Pickup: 75th Anniversary Thunderbolt Split Single-Coil Precision Bass
  • Controls: Master Volume, Master Tone
  • Strings: Fender USA 7250M Nickel Plated Steel (.045-.105)

item.title: Fernandes Revolver Bass, Metallic Purple, MIJ, 1990s, FRB-60, Original Case — $504.77
item.description:
$504.77 · Very Good · String Switch Noise

1990s, FRB-60 model


Revolver Bass


Looking stunning in a Metallic Sparkle Purple finish


Maple neck


Rosewood fretboard


Full scale, 34” bass


Super modern and flat 14” radius


Medium jumbo, 21frets


All Black hardware


Four a side, sealed headstock tuners


Four saddle adjustable bridge


P/J pickup configuration


PB-602 +JB-482 pickup models


Versatile tone, capable of most sounds and styles


Volume, Volume, Tone controls


Very light and comfortable, excellent for long playing sessions


A great quality and affordable players bass


Includes the original Fernades gig case bag






ITEM CONDITION

All machinery, electrical, and pickups are without issue. Surface marks and scratches, some impressions/dings/dimples/dents/chips, some clear coat chips (not in colour finish) to bottom body edges, some light aging and wear to hardware - as pictured.






POSTAGE & SHIPPING NOTE:

- Upon cleared payment, all small items (pedals, wiring etc) are usually posted within 24-72hr


- Guitars and Basses are packed on Monday (courier collection Tuesday) and Thursday (for courier collection Friday)


Meaning, if your purchase is on a Thursday (after 1pm), Friday, Saturday, Sunday, or Monday (before 1pm) it will be couriered Tuesday.

If your purchase is on Monday (after 1pm), Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday (before 1pm) it will be couriered Friday.


We take great care and pride in making sure guitars are carefully set up and packed correctly for transport, so we do not rush this step.




The String Switch Noise store operates primarily as an online business.

However, we do have a dedicated showroom 30min south of the Brisbane CBD.

By appointment, we are available Monday-Friday 10am-5pm (and Saturday and Sunday by availability) to test out and pick up purchased items.

Please call or contact us directly through the website.

String Switch Noise



---Shipping---

--- International delivery is possible for most items and can be arranged at exact cost of shipping. However, there are some restrictions with regions and package size. Please contact us first BEFORE making any purchase to check if an item can be delivered, and to organise the ACTUAL delivery costs. International shipping pricing (if found on listing) is an estimate only (it may be more, but it also may be less). Any applicable customs, duties, or taxes that your country may charge is solely the responsibility of you the buyer. Any questions you may have about this should be directed to your local countries authorities.

--- We proudly strive to send out all orders within 24 hours of purchase and/or receiving cleared payment (apart from holidays and weekends). However please allow up to 72hrs for packing and courier pickups. Once your item is packed you will receive notification and relevant tracking number and courier/postal details.

--- Small items such as effect pedals and wiring harnesses are sent via Australia Post standard service, with tracking ONLY. Both domestically and internationally. No responsibility will be taken from us for the loss or damage of an item by Australia Post. At your request, signature upon delivery or postal insurance can be added for the additional cost. Please contact us before purchase, or immediately after purchase.

--- Australia wide, guitars are sent via courier delivery with full insurance, tracking, and signature upon delivery services. Guitar packages MAY be sent via Australia Post if the delivery area is in a remote region/outside normal courier delivery areas/outside normal costs (and the package size is under 105cm long).

--- Courier deliveries can NOT be sent to a PO Box, Parcel Locker, or Parcel Collect service. They must be sent to a physical address, where someone can sign for the item on arrival.

--- All items are packed to the highest degree for transit. Proudly, our packaging is at the highest of standards, with the best reputation you will find anywhere in the industry. This comes from 3500+ guitars (and counting) being sent without issue both Australia wide and internationally over 10+ years (and counting). A great amount of pride and detail goes into making sure every item arrives safe and in a timely manner. Pedals and small items are packed in multiple layering of bubble wrap and double corrugated shipping cardboard. Guitars, pending the instrument, have strings de tuned so there is no tension on the neck and headstock. Neck/body /headstock are wrapped separately in multiple bubble wrap layers, high density foam and/or cardboard reinforced. If applicable it is then placed in gig/soft case or hard case. If in gig/soft case it is once again bubble wrapped/foam reinforced before being boxed. Each package/box is custom cut and fit in heavy duty double corrugated shipping cardboard. All voids are padded out and the body edge, base, and top of head stock foam reinforced/padded. Finally, the boxing will have copious amounts of high visibility “Fragile” and “This way up” warning labels.

--- We are also extremely environmentally conscious. We re use and recycle almost all bubble wrap, foam, and cardboard packaging materials.




---INFO & TERMS---

--- We do not accept any trade, trade ins, consignment, or direct customer purchases.

--- All payments to be made in full at time of purchase. The are no holds, deposits, or layby.

--- Payment can be made by Paypal, Credit Card (through Paypal), Bank Deposit/Online Transfer, Afterpay, or ZIP.

--- If payment is made via Paypal goods and service, the item will ONLY be posted/couriered to your registered Paypal address (NO alternative address). This is to ensure the Paypal buyer and seller insurance protection is valid.

--- All photos are our own and are of the actual item being offered for purchase.

We NEVER use stock photos.

An extensive amount of time and effort is put in to accurately show the items true condition, colour, and shape. Though please realise the limitations of photography. Images and colours can be perceived differently depending of the type and quality of the device it is viewed on.

--- Advertised photos form a large part of the representation of the items condition. Please view the photos carefully as well as reading all item descriptions and/or condition text. Whilst every effort is made to photograph all parts of items, not every little dimple/micro scratch can or will be photographed. We always photograph any and all large cosmetic flaws, such as paint chips/cracks/large dents, that we consider to be significant.

--- Cosmetically, items are as per detailed photos and/or descriptions. Functionally, all pots, jacks, switch and machinery components are working without issue, and serviced upon arrival. Each item is tested and/or serviced/set up before listing. It is also tested again moments before packaging, after purchase, to insure it is 100%.

---Guitars and basses are set up with action at a standard with no playability issues, unless otherwise described. However please understand no one set-up is right for everybody. If you prefer a different action height/tuning/string gauge please adjust at your own liking. Please also realise that almost all items are used, and most are 20-30+ year vintage instruments/effects. They will exhibit some wear and tear. If you need detailed specifics, extra information/on the bench specs can be given about anything you require. Please do not hesitate to ask if this is necessary.

--- No refund will be given for change of mind. There will be no warranty or refund given other than dead on arrival, broken, or faulty that is NOT already described and outlined in the descriptive text and detailed photos. In the HIGHLY unlikely event, there is damage in transit, a postal/courier insurance claim will be made to recover the cost of damage. However, we are always happy to help and work through any issue, in the rare event that one arises.

item.title: momose MJ1-ADZ-RD/R (WH/OIL-MH) — $1,978.14
item.description:
$1,978.14 · Brand New · Ikebe Music

Our Business Hours is Mon to Fri / 11:00 to 18:00 JPN time
We will close Sat and Sun, so we will reply to inquiry that received weekend on next week.
Your understanding will be appreciated.

We are receiving the customer order with first comes first serves basis both on website and at real shop.
So occasionally it will be sold out while having email communication.
Please understand in advance.

Japanese consumption tax is not required for international shopping. However you may be required to pay duty, tax and import fee when receiving your items in your country. You must pay all these duty, tax and fee as receiver/importer and we do not cover any of them.

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

This is a special edition MJ model featuring anodized parts. Based on the MJ1/R, which uses an alder body and rosewood fingerboard combination, this limited edition model has been customized with a red anodized pickguard.

The oil finish paint job uses a white oil finish that makes the parts colors stand out. It features a matching headstock design, and the brand logo is also the same color as the parts, creating a unified design.

- Wider horn section for improved playability in higher positions
The MJ model features a deeper cut on the treble-side horn compared to typical JB-type basses. This provides excellent playability, allowing even players with larger hands to easily access the higher frets.

- Recessed joint plate processing: While employing an orthodox plate joint that brings out the ideal sound, the playability is further enhanced by recessing the plate portion.

• Wood binding to prevent fretboard problems: The sides of the fretboard are made of the same material as the fretboard. This not only provides a smooth fretboard surface but also prevents problems such as fret burrs popping out after years of use.

- Original Gravitas pickups
The Gravitas pickups feature readjusted pole piece heights and optimized tone balance for each string. The thick sound from low to mid-range adds even more depth and presence to the tone.

• Equipped with a "Turbo Switch" that produces a powerful, thick tone by pulling up (raising) the Turbo Switch tone pot. Despite being a passive circuit, it expands the range of tones and enhances the player's expressiveness and creativity.

- Luminlay side dots: All models feature Luminlay side position markers, a phosphorescent material that stores light and releases it in the dark. This is highly valued on stages in live music venues and concert halls where low-light lighting effects, such as blackouts, are common, and it is a feature that is popular among professional musicians. It reduces the risk of losing sight of your hands in the dark.

The GOTOH GBU510C-12 tuning pegs are used as the base model's tuning pegs. Their high-precision construction by GOTOH and 20:1 gear ratio ensure stable tuning.

- Features medium jumbo-sized Jescar FW55090NS (※9662NS) frets with a fret height of 1.4mm and a width of 2.28mm.

• Puretone Jack: All models feature the Puretone jack, renowned for its secure hold and low noise. Unlike conventional jacks, which have one hot terminal and two cold terminals, the Puretone jack securely holds the plug from both sides. This ensures that the plug remains stable even after prolonged use, supporting the player's performance activities.

・Centralab Oil Capacitors: Along with the development of the new pickup, we also reviewed the capacitors. From among many candidates, we selected Centralab oil capacitors from the perspective of pickups and other factors. They offer excellent responsiveness to the player's nuances and a bright sound that comes forward, creating a new Momose tone.

• A fingerboard with a 310R radius that is flatter than vintage-style fingerboards, allowing for a wider range of playing styles. This makes it possible to set the string height lower than ever before, making it easier to try technical playing styles that require fast fretting.

*The image is a sample. Due to the nature of the product, the wood grain and color will vary from piece to piece.

Body: Alder 2P
Neck: Maple
Fingerboard: Indian Rosewood
Frets: Jescar FW55090NS (9662NS)
Nut: Oiled Bone
Machineheads: GOTOH GBU-510C-12
Bridge: GOTOH 203B-4
Pickups: Gravitas-J set
Controls: 2Vol, 1Tone (Pull up: Turbo SW)
Scale: 864mm (34 inches)
Width at Nut: 38.0mm
Neck Grip: C Shape
Radius: 310R
Finish: Top Lacquer
Strings: Elixir NANOWEB(45-105)

Gig bag included

• Equipped with a "Turbo Switch" that produces a powerful, thick tone by pulling up (raising) the Turbo Switch tone pot. Despite being a passive circuit, it expands the range of tones and enhances the player's expressiveness and creativity.

- Luminlay side dots: All models feature Luminlay side position markers, a phosphorescent material that stores light and releases it in the dark. This is highly valued on stages in live music venues and concert halls where low-light lighting effects, such as blackouts, are common, and it is a feature that is popular among professional musicians. It reduces the risk of losing sight of your hands in the dark.

The GOTOH GBU510C-12 tuning pegs are used as the base model's tuning pegs. Their high-precision construction by GOTOH and 20:1 gear ratio ensure stable tuning.

- Features medium jumbo-sized Jescar FW55090NS (※9662NS) frets with a fret height of 1.4mm and a width of 2.28mm.

• Puretone Jack: All models feature the Puretone jack, renowned for its secure hold and low noise. Unlike conventional jacks, which have one hot terminal and two cold terminals, the Puretone jack securely holds the plug from both sides. This ensures that the plug remains stable even after prolonged use, supporting the player's performance activities.

・Centralab Oil Capacitors: Along with the development of the new pickup, we also reviewed the capacitors. From among many candidates, we selected Centralab oil capacitors from the perspective of pickups and other factors. They offer excellent responsiveness to the player's nuances and a bright sound that comes forward, creating a new Momose tone.

• A fingerboard with a 310R radius that is flatter than vintage-style fingerboards, allowing for a wider range of playing styles. This makes it possible to set the string height lower than ever before, making it easier to try technical playing styles that require fast fretting.

*The image is a sample. Due to the nature of the product, the wood grain and color will vary from piece to piece.

Body: Alder 2P
Neck: Maple
Fingerboard: Indian Rosewood
Frets: Jescar FW55090NS (9662NS)
Nut: Oiled Bone
Machineheads: GOTOH GBU-510C-12
Bridge: GOTOH 203B-4
Pickups: Gravitas-J set
Controls: 2Vol, 1Tone (Pull up: Turbo SW)
Scale: 864mm (34 inches)
Width at Nut: 38.0mm
Neck Grip: C Shape
Radius: 310R
Finish: Top Lacquer
Strings: Elixir NANOWEB(45-105)

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

Momose Original Series MJ
The MJ is an original model that inherits the traditional JB style while incorporating Momose's unique refinements. Each guitar is handcrafted by artisans at the Asuka factory, from woodworking to painting and assembly. Its exquisite craftsmanship results in high playability, which has earned it the support of top players both domestically and internationally, providing unwavering quality that only Japanese-made instruments can offer. The MJ2/R features a combination of an ash body and a rosewood fingerboard.

- Wider horn section for improved playability in higher positions
The MJ model features a deeper cut on the treble-side horn compared to typical JB-type basses. This provides excellent playability, allowing even players with larger hands to easily access the higher frets.

- Recessed joint plate processing: While employing an orthodox plate joint that brings out the ideal sound, the playability is further enhanced by recessing the plate portion.

• Wood binding to prevent fretboard problems: The sides of the fretboard are made of the same material as the fretboard. This not only provides a smooth fretboard surface but also prevents problems such as fret burrs popping out after years of use.

- Original Gravitas pickups
The Gravitas pickups feature readjusted pole piece heights and optimized tone balance for each string. The thick sound from low to mid-range adds even more depth and presence to the tone.

• Equipped with a "Turbo Switch" that produces a powerful, thick tone by pulling up (raising) the Turbo Switch tone pot. Despite being a passive circuit, it expands the range of tones and enhances the player's expressiveness and creativity.

- Luminlay side dots: All models feature Luminlay side position markers, a phosphorescent material that stores light and releases it in the dark. This is highly valued on stages in live music venues and concert halls where low-light lighting effects, such as blackouts, are common, and it is a feature that is popular among professional musicians. It reduces the risk of losing sight of your hands in the dark.

The GOTOH GBU510C-12 tuning pegs are used as the base model's tuning pegs. Their high-precision construction by GOTOH and 20:1 gear ratio ensure stable tuning.

- Features medium jumbo-sized Jescar FW55090NS (※9662NS) frets with a fret height of 1.4mm and a width of 2.28mm.

• Puretone Jack: All models feature the Puretone(R) jack, renowned for its secure jack hold and low noise. Unlike conventional jacks which have one hot terminal and two cold terminals, the Puretone jack has two hot terminals and two cold terminals, securely holding the plug from both sides. This ensures that the plug does not loosen easily even after prolonged use, supporting the player's performance activities with stable performance.

・Centralab Oil Capacitors: Along with the development of the new pickup, we also reviewed the capacitors. From among many candidates, we selected Centralab oil capacitors from the perspective of pickups and other factors. They offer excellent responsiveness to the player's nuances and a bright sound that comes forward, creating a new Momose tone.

• A fingerboard with a 310R radius that is flatter than vintage-style fingerboards, allowing for a wider range of playing styles. This makes it possible to set the string height lower than ever before, making it easier to try technical playing styles that require fast fretting.

*The image is a sample. Due to the nature of the product, the wood grain and color will vary from piece to piece.

item.title: Infinite Trad JB 4st (Deep Lake Placid Blue/Light Aged) — $2,701.16
item.description:
$2,701.16 · Brand New · Ikebe Music

Our Business Hours is Mon to Fri / 11:00 to 18:00 JPN time
We will close Sat and Sun, so we will reply to inquiry that received weekend on next week.
Your understanding will be appreciated.

We are receiving the customer order with first comes first serves basis both on website and at real shop.
So occasionally it will be sold out while having email communication.
Please understand in advance.

Japanese consumption tax is not required for international shopping. However you may be required to pay duty, tax and import fee when receiving your items in your country. You must pay all these duty, tax and fee as receiver/importer and we do not cover any of them.

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

"Infinite" is a cutting-edge brand that combines traditional styles with unique design and sound.

"Infinite" is a custom brand brought to you by Osaka-based repair workshop "High-End Music." The models are created by incorporating the woodworking and assembly techniques cultivated in the repair workshop, as well as a wealth of unique ideas developed through constant research and development, resulting in player-specific models that cannot be found in other brands.

Already highly acclaimed for their electric guitars, Infinite has restocked their highly anticipated bass guitar. This high-quality model combines standard specifications with Infinite's unique attention to detail and our store's aesthetic requirements. While the alder body and maple neck offer standard specs, the unique neck molding and jointing method create a punchy, full-bodied sound that sets it apart from so-called vintage guitars. The optional Honduras rosewood fingerboard adds a rich color and firmness, resulting in a crisp, responsive attack. The rich, powerful sound is incredibly satisfying to play, resulting in a distinctive sound. The unique body with a deep cutaway, head shape, and BFTS technology for precise intonation contribute to the guitar's distinctive sound and playability. The body, finished with a lacquer top and the brand's signature aged finish, exudes a warmth and texture, reminiscent of a well-played instrument.

While inheriting traditional style, this brand is sure to make a splash in the future scene with its unique design and sound. Be sure to try out its thick, powerful sound.

■Features・KogicLogic
The three convex joints on the neck and the three concave joints on the body fit together perfectly to provide rich sustain. Bolt joints are also less likely to shift center due to shocks, maintaining a stable condition.

A unique method for bonding the fingerboard and neck: grooves are carved into the fingerboard and a convex part is formed on the neck. The increased bonding area promotes integration between the neck and fingerboard, and the convex part on the neck side increases the neck's strength. As a result, the neck firmly absorbs string vibrations, transmitting them to the Kogiku Logic.

・Jescar stainless steel frets
Jescar stainless steel frets are standard, and the side edges are finished with a rounded, smooth edge developed through high-end music repairs. The highly durable, stress-free neck provides full support for playability.

・BFTS Installation The Bass Feiten Tuning System (BFTS) is installed as standard on all models. The fret grooves and nut grooves are formed using a CNC router, allowing the scale position to be machined with precision, and BFTS is installed to further improve pitch accuracy. (*Adjustments can be made using a standard tuner. If you would like precise re-setting when changing strings or adjusting string height, please consult with a store.)

- We repeatedly tried and tested the headstock and tuning peg position, and achieved the sound balance possible with the sloped headstock shape, including the position of the posts and the depth of the tuning pegs. To prioritize tuning, we eliminated the string guide and adjusted the sound by adjusting the nut shape and post position.

Unprecedented accessibility: Access to the high positions is smoother than ever before, something unimaginable with existing neck shapes. Extending the joint toward the body end improves access, and the wider joint area provides excellent sustain and a stronger sense of unity between the body and neck.

*The image of the inside of the joint is a sample.

Body wood: Selected Alder 2P
Neck Wood: Maple
Finderboard wood: Maple
Radius: 10-14R
Neck shape: Mid C
Fret/Nut: 57110-S/Bone
Pickup : Infinite Original PU 60's
Control: 2V1T

Gig bag included
